name: gnutls-bin
title: gnutls-bin
description:
GNU TLS library - commandline utilities
GnuTLS is a portable library which implements the Transport Layer
Security (TLS 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3) and Datagram
Transport Layer Security (DTLS 1.0, 1.2) protocols.
.
GnuTLS features support for:
- certificate path validation, as well as DANE and trust on first use.
- the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP).
- public key methods, including RSA and Elliptic curves, as well as password
and key authentication methods such as SRP and PSK protocols.
- all the strong encryption algorithms, including AES and Camellia.
- CPU-assisted cryptography with VIA padlock and AES-NI instruction sets.
- HSMs and cryptographic tokens, via PKCS #11.
.
This package contains a commandline interface to the GNU TLS library, which
can be used to set up secure connections from e.g. shell scripts, debugging
connection issues or managing certificates.
.
Useful utilities include:
- TLS termination: gnutls-cli, gnutls-serv
- key and certificate management: certtool, ocsptool, p11tool
- credential management: srptool, psktool

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use certtool because GNU TLS library - commandline utilities.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use certtool as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `certtool [-flags] [-flag [value]] [--option-name[[=| ]value]]` as documented upstream for certtool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `certtool --generate-privkey --outfile key.pem --rsa` as documented upstream for certtool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `certtool --generate-request --load-privkey key.pem    --outfile request.pem` as documented upstream for certtool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./certtool --generate-request --load-privkey "pkcs11:..."   --load-pubkey "pkcs11:..." --outfile request.pem` as documented upstream for certtool.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair certtool with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `certtool --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install gnutls28. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `certtool [-flags] [-flag [value]] [--option-name[[=| ]value]]`. Further invocations from upstream: `certtool --generate-privkey --outfile key.pem --rsa`; `certtool --generate-request --load-privkey key.pem    --outfile request.pem`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: GNU TLS library - commandline utilities.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: GNU TLS library - commandline utilities.
